mission.title: kube-vip: svc_election=true will cause load balancers with the same IP to appear on different servers
mission.description: svc_election=true will cause load balancers with the same IP to appear on different servers. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current kube-vip deployment
steps.description:
Verify your kube-vip version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kube-vip -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kube-vip
helm list -n kube-vip 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working kube-vip installation.

steps.title: Review kube-vip configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kube-vip configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kube-vip -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kube-vip
kubectl get configmap -n kube-vip -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kube-vip
```
I have a three-node Kubernetes cluster with two load balancers using the same IP but different port numbers. This VIP appears on two nodes simultaneously.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1.

steps.title: Apply the fix for svc_election=true will cause load balancers with the same…
steps.description:
This should fix #879

This PR introduces new annotation `kube-vip.io/leaseName: <name>` that will tie multiple services with single lease (so only one node will be a leader)..

Additionally fixed some issues I found with IP address deletion in ARP mode.

Edit:

Added support for leader election in
```yaml
apiVersion: v1
data:
  cidr-qt-system: 10.106.109.9/32
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  annotations:
    meta.helm.sh/release-name: kube-vip-cloud-provider
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/managed-by: Helm
  name: kubevip
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kube-vip -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kube-vip
kubectl get events -n kube-vip --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "svc_election=true will cause load balancers with the same…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
This should fix #879

This PR introduces new annotation `kube-vip.io/leaseName: <name>` that will tie multiple services with single lease (so only one node will be a leader)..

Additionally fixed some issues I found with IP address deletion in ARP mode.

Edit:

Added support for leader election in BGP mode and e2e tests for RT and BGP leader election with common lease.
codeSnippets:
apiVersion: v1
data:
  cidr-qt-system: 10.106.109.9/32
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  annotations:
    meta.helm.sh/release-name: kube-vip-cloud-provider
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/managed-by: Helm
  name: kubevip

apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  annotations:
    kube-vip.io/loadbalancerIPs: 10.106.109.9
  labels:
    implementation: kube-vip
  name: traefik-lb-1
spec:
  ports:
  - name: agent
    port: 8000
    protocol: TCP
    targetPort: agent
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: traefik
    app.kubernetes.io/name: traefik
  type: LoadBalancer

apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  annotations:
    kube-vip.io/loadbalancerIPs: 10.106.109.9
  labels:
    implementation: kube-vip
  name: traefik-lb-2
spec:
  ports:
  - name: web
    port: 80
    protocol: TCP
    targetPort: web
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: traefik
    app.kubernetes.io/name: traefik
  type: LoadBalancer

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kube-vip installed or the issue environment reproducible.
